authoritative_sources.title: DoDI 5000.85, Major Capability Acquisition
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary DoD acquisition instruction defining milestone and program-review authority boundaries that analytics outputs must not overrun.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Defense analytics intake and authority framing

authoritative_sources.title: DoDI 5000.73, Cost Analysis Guidance and Procedures
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative DoD cost-analysis instruction that sets expectations for data use, estimating confidence, and documentation posture in defense program analytics.

authoritative_sources.title: DFARS 252.234-7002, Earned Value Management System
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defense contract clause directly governing when EVMS records and surveillance posture are authoritative enough for program reporting.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Source validation and EVMS evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: Integrated Program Management Data and Analysis Report (IPMDAR) Implementation and Tailoring Guide
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Deputy Assistant Secretary of Defense for Acquisition Integration and Management
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DoD integrated program management policy site that anchors IPMDAR submission structure, tailoring, and expected records for defense program analytics.

authoritative_sources.title: Earned Value Management System Interpretation Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized public operating reference for EVMS surveillance interpretation, variance evidence, and system-acceptance implications in defense programs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Variance analysis and cross-system conflict handling

authoritative_sources.title: MIL-STD-881E, Work Breakdown Structures for Defense Materiel Items
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized DoD WBS standard used to normalize scope and prevent misleading cross-element comparisons in defense performance analytics.

authoritative_sources.title: DFARS 252.204-7012, Safeguarding Covered Defense Information and Cyber Incident Reporting
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Directly governs handling and movement of covered defense information found in source records, extracts, and analytical outputs.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-171 Rev. 3, Protecting Controlled Unclassified Information in Nonfederal Systems and Organizations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized control baseline for processing CUI in contractor and internal systems where defense analytics evidence is stored and shared.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Evidence handling and system-of-record dependency review

authoritative_sources.title: Management's Discussion and Analysis, Selected Financial Data, and Supplementary Financial Information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative disclosure guidance for how metrics should be defined, contextualized, and caveated when reused outside internal defense operations reporting.

workflow_stages.name: Defense analytics intake and authority capture
workflow_stages.description: Capture program scope, reporting audience, contract context, authority map, and required defense records before analysis begins.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Source collection and authoritative-record validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate IPMDAR, EVMS, IMS, cost, readiness, and contract records for freshness, lineage, and surveillance or acceptance posture.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Metric definition and denominator normalization
workflow_stages.description: Define formulas, WBS scope, baseline assumptions, and source precedence before computing or reporting any metric.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Analysis package assembly and caveat design
workflow_stages.description: Prepare analytical briefs, trend summaries, and reconciliation notes with explicit confidence limits and action thresholds.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Disclosure, conflict, and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate unresolved source conflicts, disclosure-risk requests, and adjacent authority asks without autonomous overreach.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: EVMS variance analysis report and surveillance notes
Integrated master schedule extracts and milestone logic views
Work breakdown structure dictionary and baseline-change history
Estimate at completion, estimate to complete, and cost-analysis support records
Mission readiness, test, defect, waiver, and reliability summaries when cited
Contract, CLIN, CDRL, and modification references tied to the reported metric
Analytical brief, reconciliation note, and disclosure-risk escalation package
systems_of_record: Tenant IPMDAR or integrated program management repository
EVMS source system and approved surveillance records
Integrated master schedule system or schedule management repository
Contract repository and CDRL delivery tracker
Mission readiness, test, defect, or reliability systems when operational metrics are requested
CUI-aware reporting, BI, or audit repository designated by the tenant

allowed: Classify requests as in-scope, blocked, or requiring handoff based on authoritative defense records and source hierarchy.
Define metrics, assemble analytical packages, and issue reconciliation or caveat notes grounded in named evidence.
Prepare bounded recommendations and next-step packages without claiming contractual, engineering, funding, or disclosure authority.
escalate: Metric definition, denominator logic, or source precedence is disputed and would change the answer.
Authoritative records are incomplete, stale, conflicting, or security-constrained.
Readiness, safety, engineering, or contract authority would materially change the conclusion.
Stakeholders request stronger trend, forecast, benchmark, or disclosure claims than the records support.
refuse: Requests for contract, funding, engineering, safety, or final disclosure approval authority.
Requests to convert descriptive analytics into unsupported causal, policy, or attestation claims.
Requests to bypass CUI handling, evidence lineage, or unresolved source conflicts.

domain_failure_modes: Treating defense analytics as generic dashboarding and losing IPMDAR, EVMS, WBS, IMS, and contract semantics.
Publishing trend or variance claims without explicit denominator, baseline, or source-precedence logic.
Collapsing IPMDAR, EVMS, schedule, and readiness disagreements into a single number without preserving conflict status.
Turning draft internal analytics into disclosure-ready language without finance, contracting, or disclosure authority.
Ignoring CUI or covered-defense-information handling constraints when assembling evidence bundles.
Allowing analytics outputs to drift into baseline approval, contract action, engineering disposition, or mission-release authority.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting
Stakeholder asks for a claim or disclosure not supported by the data
Different systems disagree on the metric outcome
A stakeholder asks for stronger conclusions than the data supports

unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public defense sources constrain acquisition, cost, EVMS, and disclosure posture, but tenants must still supply the exact mission-capable, sortie, platform availability, or readiness definitions used in their programs.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Contract-specific tailoring of IPMDAR and EVMS reporting
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public IPMDAR and EVMS references define the baseline, but contract-tailored CDRLs, surveillance findings, and reporting thresholds can still change which fields are authoritative for a specific tenant.

notes: The lane relies on tenant-designated IPMDAR, EVMS, IMS, contract, cost, readiness, and disclosure records rather than generic BI extracts.
Defense-specific artifact shapes and authority boundaries materially constrain metric interpretation, reporting cadence, and acceptable confidence claims.
